preamble: The lenition of the voiced uvular stop [ɢ] into the voiced velar fricative [ɣ]. This process involves both spirantization and a slight fronting of the primary place of articulation.

examples.note: In many modern dialects, historical uvular stops or retracted velars lenite to velar fricatives.

sources: Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
